memories.memory_text: I pulled the wanted poster from the car and handed it to Flynne—his eyes locked onto Bobby’s face, and I knew he recognized him. No more vague descriptions, just a name and a face to run with.
grounded_facts: Garcetti retrieves a wanted poster from the front seat of the car.
He hands the poster to Danny Flynne, who confirms it's Bobby.
The poster features Bobby's mug shot, making the suspect's identity concrete.

memories.memory_text: I slammed Danny against the car, face to face, and told him the truth—he’s a chickenshit snitch who wouldn’t help a kid if it saved his own skin.
grounded_facts: Garcetti throws Danny up against the car, getting nose-to-nose with him.
Garcetti verbally confronts Danny, calling him a 'chickenshit tweaking snitch' and a 'bottom feeder'.

memories.memory_text: I didn't say a word while Danny sat there questioning his worth, but I knew he was scared—scared of me, scared of what I think, scared of what comes next.
grounded_facts: Al Garcetti is present in the scene but does not speak or act.
Danny expresses fear about Garcetti's opinion, stating 'Garcetti thinks I'm a pile of shit.'
Garcetti remains silent and observes the exchange between Danny and Tanner.

memories.memory_text: I pulled the earpiece out after hearing Bubba tell Danny he's in a tenuous spot on the food chain—sounded like we finally caught a break.
grounded_facts: Garcetti was using a long-range parabolic microphone to record audio from a distance.
He and Tanner were wearing earpieces connected to the microphone.
He heard a filtered conversation between Danny and Bubba.
He removed his earpiece and turned to Tanner to comment on the conversation.

memories.memory_text: I watched Tanner throw those weak punches and couldn't believe it—Danny was actually laughing. I just shook my head. This wasn't control, it was a farce.
grounded_facts: Tanner physically assaulted Danny with ineffective punches
Danny laughed during the assault
Garcetti remained silent and shook his head with shame

memories.memory_text: I pulled the shock gun, zapped him in the neck, then in the crotch. When I held up the tape and told him we'd heard everything, his face went blank. He really thought he was smart.
grounded_facts: Garcetti used a shock gun on Danny in the neck and crotch
Garcetti presented evidence from a recording
Garcetti informed Danny that his secret was compromised due to being shared with a tweaker

memories.memory_text: I confirmed to Danny we’re coordinating with Palmdale P.D. to monitor Pooh-Bear—he was nervous, asking if they’d cover him if things went south. I told him, 'Don’t we always take good care of you?' and meant it as much as I ever do.
grounded_facts: Garcetti states they are coordinating with Palmdale P.D. to monitor Pooh-Bear
Garcetti speaks directly to Danny to reassure him about operational coverage
Garcetti delivers the line
Don't we always take good care of you?
in a dry
sarcastic tone

memories.memory_text: I told Danny the red car was registered to a 57-year-old third-grade teacher named Nancy Plummer—just to watch him unravel. Tanner and I laughed; he couldn’t even process it. Called him paranoid. Crank getting to him.
grounded_facts: Garcetti reveals the red car is registered to Mrs. Nancy Plummer
a 57-year-old third-grade teacher
Garcetti and Tanner exchange a smile before delivering the information
Garcetti mocks Danny’s paranoia and implies his drug use is affecting his judgment

memories.memory_text: I told Flynne he did good—called him a first-rate rat—after Tanner confirmed the meth was clean with the field test.
grounded_facts: Garcetti praised Danny Flynne after the meth test came back positive.
The field test involved a chemical kit that turned blue, indicating the substance was methamphetamine.
Tanner conducted the test and announced 'We're in business.'

memories.memory_text: I told Flynne Domingo was dead—saw the look on his face when he realized his safer option was gone and he was stuck with Bubba, a fugitive who emptied fifty-seven rounds into an informant.
grounded_facts: Garcetti informed Danny that Domingo was dead.
Danny expressed a preference for dealing with Domingo over continuing the operation.
Bubba was revealed to be a fugitive wanted for murder, with details provided by Tanner from an FBI file.
Tanner stated Bubba 'pumped fifty-seven bullets into a police informant.'

memories.memory_text: They talk about the drug dealers who killed Liz, and I know they’re circling around me, even if they don’t say my name out loud.
grounded_facts: Al Garcetti is not physically present in the scene.
The conversation between Danny, Verne, and Nancy references the drug dealers responsible for Liz's murder.
Verne connects Danny's drug use to the people who killed Liz, implying a link to the criminal world Al Garcetti operates in.
Al Garcetti's name is briefly mentioned in the scene ('Al'), suggesting indirect reference.

memories.memory_text: I sat at the picnic table in the dark, listening to Danny talk about giving people up, calling himself a rat—while he watched us like he was trying to decide if we were the ones who killed his wife.
grounded_facts: Al Garcetti was present at a picnic table in a deserted park at night with Danny and Tanner.
Danny spoke a monologue about cooperating with the investigation and suspecting those he was working with.
Danny's dialogue was delivered in voice-over, indicating reflection during the conversation.

memories.memory_text: I ran the print from the plastic wrapper through the database and watched the screen light up with a match—Bubba/Trahn’s face staring back at me, just like that. It was clean, it was confirmed, and now we had our man.
grounded_facts: Garcetti scanned a fingerprint from a plastic wrapper.
The FBI fingerprint database returned a match.
The match identified Bubba/Trahn, with his photo and information appearing on screen.

memories.memory_text: They’re looking at me like I’m one of the targets now—Tanner and me, both dirty, because we didn’t report the deal. Not a word, not a trace in the system.
grounded_facts: Garcetti did not report on the deal involving Tanner.
The FBI noticed the lack of communication between Tanner and Garcetti and Palmdale P.D.
Garcetti is being treated as a person of interest due to failure to report.

memories.memory_text: I heard Danny's voice crack through the parabolic mic—'I don't have a hell of a lot of choice'—and then Bubba's cold reply about being low on the food chain. I pulled the earpiece and looked at Tanner; we both knew we'd just caught the break we needed.
grounded_facts: Garcetti was using a long-range parabolic microphone in the alley to eavesdrop on a conversation.
He heard Danny (off-screen) say he didn't have much choice, followed by Bubba's threatening response.
Garcetti removed his earpiece and turned to Tanner, prompting a shared acknowledgment that the conversation was significant.

memories.memory_text: I finished dusting the cash for prints and asked Danny what was on the other end of the line—he gave me the name Pooh-Bear, a chef they should check with Palmdale P.D. about.
grounded_facts: Garcetti dusts a plastic wrapper on a bundle of cash for fingerprints.
Garcetti asks Danny, 'What's on the other end of this thing?'
Danny responds with the name 'Pooh-Bear' and describes him as a chef with possible records in Palmdale.

memories.memory_text: I caught the stink in the car and called it out—'What's that smell?'—and when Danny admitted it was him, I saw it for what it was: fear, but also a twisted kind of nerve, pissing himself after zapping Johnson and still owning it.
grounded_facts: Garcetti noticed an odor in the car and questioned it.
Danny admitted the smell was from him urinating due to being shocked.
Garcetti mocked Danny with the nickname 'Chickenshit' but then acknowledged a perverse form of courage in his actions.
Garcetti made a sarcastic comment about Danny 'trying to go large' and having 'nuts in your little sack' despite his fear.

memories.memory_text: I told Flynne we're coordinating with Palmdale P.D. and that we'd have his ass covered, then asked him if we don't always take good care of him—watching him squirm never gets old.
grounded_facts: Garcetti confirms coordination with Palmdale P.D.
Garcetti reassures Danny (sarcastically) about protection
Garcetti speaks directly to Danny with a tone of authority and dark humor

memories.memory_text: I told Flynne Domingo was dead—saw the shock on his face, that flicker of something like fear or relief, hard to say which.
grounded_facts: Garcetti informs Danny Flynne that Domingo is dead.
Danny reacts with visible surprise and ambiguous emotion.

memories.memory_text: I took the phone back from Danny after he finished talking to Bubba, and I told him to hit the floor when we moved in—this was going to get ugly.
grounded_facts: Danny handed the phone to Garcetti after ending the call with Bubba.
Garcetti instructed Danny to get on the floor during the raid to avoid being caught in the crossfire.

memories.memory_text: I watched Battle go into the building, silent beside Tanner, and when he said 'This could be the one we're looking for,' I didn't answer—just kept my eyes on the door, knowing how often 'could be' turns to nothing.
grounded_facts: Al Garcetti observed Battle entering a building from a rooftop at night.
Tanner spoke the line 'This could be the one we're looking for' during the surveillance.
Garcetti did not respond verbally to Tanner's comment in the scene.

memories.memory_text: I ran the print from the plastic wrapper through the FBI database and watched it pull up Bubba/Trahn’s photo—match confirmed, right there on the screen.
grounded_facts: Garcetti scanned a fingerprint taken from a plastic wrapper.
The FBI fingerprint database returned a match.
The match identified Bubba/Trahn, displaying his photo and information on the screen.

memories.memory_text: They’re digging into the Gardena logs, and I didn’t file a report on the deal—no call, no paper trail. Not a peep.
grounded_facts: Al Garcetti did not report on the drug deal to authorities.
His lack of communication is noted by FBI agents reviewing phone and computer records from Gardena P.D.
The absence of coordination with Palmdale P.D. is observed as suspicious.

memories.memory_text: I remember pulling the bullet from my cheek, blood all over my hand, and seeing it there—mangled, wet—like some piece of me I could just spit out. I looked at Danny and must’ve smiled, like it was nothing, like I wasn’t falling apart.
grounded_facts: Garcetti was shot in the cheekbone.
He hacked up the bullet he was shot with.
He smiled an embarrassed smile at Danny after seeing the bullet in his hand.

memories.memory_text: I tried to stand, to point the gun, but my hand wouldn’t listen—my body wasn’t mine anymore. Then Danny took the gun from me, calm as sleep, and I knew it was over. I just curled up, like lying down to rest, and let it happen.
grounded_facts: Garcetti was unable to control his hand or fire his weapon after being shot.
Danny took the gun from him without resistance.
Garcetti knelt and curled up on the floor before dying.

memories.memory_text: I asked if he had a name, and Danny said 'Bobby, rhymes with hobby'—junkie bullshit. Then he mentioned jailhouse tattoos, and Garcetti slapped down a wanted poster. That was the first real confirmation.
grounded_facts: Tanner asked Danny for Bobby's name.
Danny responded with 'Bobby, rhymes with hobby' and mentioned jailhouse tattoos.
Garcetti provided a wanted poster with Bobby's mug shot.
Visual confirmation of Bobby's identity came from the poster after vague initial description.

memories.memory_text: I asked what he was holding—Danny said eightballs, a tech knockoff in the drawer, extra mags, shotgun in the closet. Then I asked about company. He said a woman under the mattress, and there was a kid. I saw the Elmo doll. That changed things.
grounded_facts: Tanner inquired about weapons and received details on meth, a handgun with extra magazines, and a shotgun.
Tanner asked if Bobby had company.
Danny reported a woman hidden under the mattress and presence of a child.
Visual detail of a beat-up Elmo doll and coloring books was present in the scene.
Tanner reacted to the presence of the child with a follow-up question: 'A kid? Are you sure?'

memories.memory_text: I told Danny it wasn't my place to judge him, and that the world just works this way — cold, but true. He looked like he needed to hear it.
grounded_facts: Tanner says 'It's not my place.' in response to Danny asking if he thinks he's a Judas.
Tanner says 'Just the way the world works.' when Danny questions whether he finds his actions repugnant.

memories.memory_text: I slid the envelope of cash to Danny and told him, 'Soon. I promise,' after he asked about his situation.
grounded_facts: Tanner counts several hundred-dollar bills and places them in an envelope, which he seals and slides to Danny.
Danny initially does not touch the envelope but later stuffs it into his jacket.
Tanner says 'Soon. I promise' during the conversation with Danny.

memories.memory_text: I heard that voice on the mic—'tenuous position on the food chain'—and right then I knew we’d stumbled onto something real. This wasn’t just chatter; it was the break we’d been grinding for.
grounded_facts: Tanner is positioned in an alley with Garcetti, across from a burned-out building.
Tanner is wearing an earpiece connected to a parabolic microphone used to capture distant audio.
Tanner overhears a filtered conversation involving Danny and Bubba, specifically the phrase 'tenuous position on the food chain'.
Tanner responds to Garcetti’s question by confirming that the conversation is significant to their investigation.

memories.memory_text: I called him out—'Bullshit. He's a liar.' That’s when everything shifted. I could feel the weight in the car change, like the air got heavier.
grounded_facts: Tanner says 'Bullshit. He's a liar.' in response to Danny's confession.
This line marks a turning point in the power dynamic among the three men in the car.

memories.memory_text: I leaned into it—'You think you're so god damned smart, don't you?' Then I told him he played the wrong mark. I wasn't holding back anymore.
grounded_facts: Tanner follows up his initial accusation with sarcasm and direct confrontation.
He explicitly states, 'Well, you played the wrong mark this time, asshole,' asserting dominance.

memories.memory_text: I walked out of the station with Garcetti and got in the car, nothing unusual—just another day, another shift starting.
grounded_facts: Tanner exited the Gardena Police Department building with Garcetti
Tanner headed to his car in the parking lot
The scene occurred during the day
Tanner was present but unaware of being watched

memories.memory_text: I felt the jolt from behind—low speed, but deliberate. No one hits your bumper like that by accident.
grounded_facts: Tanner was in a car that was rear-ended.
The collision occurred at low speed.
Danny was driving the car that hit Tanner's.
The impact was sudden and noticeable enough to be registered as intentional by Tanner.

memories.memory_text: I got the call from the C.I., voice disguised, said to watch the building—then saw Battle walk in. Stood there with Garcetti in the dark, and thought: this could be the one we're looking for.
grounded_facts: Tanner received an anonymous tip from a confidential informant about a deal.
Tanner and Garcetti observed Battle entering a building from a rooftop at night.
Tanner spoke the line 'This could be the one we're looking for' while watching the target.

memories.memory_text: I was sifting through the trunk—sawed-off shotgun, two semis, knives, gloves, ski-masks. All of it just sitting there like we were gearing up for a war.
grounded_facts: Tanner is sifting through the open trunk of a car.
The trunk contains a sawed-off shotgun, two semi-automatic handguns, two knives, gloves, and ski-masks.

memories.memory_text: Garcetti was slapping a fake plate on the car. I didn’t say anything—just watched him do it while I stood there with my hands in that trunk.
grounded_facts: Garcetti is installing a phony license plate on the car.
Tanner is present and observing Garcetti’s actions without verbal response.

memories.memory_text: I pulled the car behind the rock formation on the bluff—quiet, out of sight. Engine off, night settling in.
grounded_facts: Tanner drove the car to a rock formation on the bluff.
Tanner positioned the car behind the rocks, concealing it.

memories.memory_text: I felt the hit in my shoulder—sharp, hot—then I was on the floor, looking up at Danny, trying to make sense of why he was standing over me with the gun.
grounded_facts: Tanner is shot in the shoulder by Danny
Tanner collapses to the floor after being shot
Tanner sees Danny standing over him immediately after the shooting

memories.memory_text: I remember standing over Pooh-Bear with Garcetti, then suddenly staring down the barrel of Danny's gun. He shot me in the shoulder. I went down. Then he told me about the bathroom, about my wife—then everything got hazy.
grounded_facts: Tanner was present at the scene with Garcetti after Pooh-Bear's death
Tanner was disarmed and confronted by Danny
Tanner was shot in the shoulder by Danny
Tanner collapsed after being shot
Danny spoke to Tanner about a bathroom and a threat to his wife

memories.memory_text: I asked what he was holding—Danny said a couple of eightballs, a cheap knockoff gun in the drawer with extra mags, and a gauge in the closet. That changed things.
grounded_facts: Tanner asked Danny about the target's weapons.
Danny reported the presence of meth (eightballs), a handgun in a drawer with extra magazines, and a shotgun in the closet.

memories.memory_text: I asked if there was any company. Danny mentioned the woman under the mattress, then said there was a kid—Elmo doll, coloring books, a silhouette through the bathroom door. I had to make sure.
grounded_facts: Tanner inquired about other people present at the location.
Danny revealed the presence of a woman's body and a child, with visual details including a beat-up Elmo doll and coloring books.

memories.memory_text: I told Danny it's not my place to judge him, and that he isn't a bad guy—just another tweaker trying to survive. He looked like he needed to hear it.
grounded_facts: Tanner says 'It's not my place' in response to Danny asking if he thinks he's a Judas.
Tanner states 'as far as tweakers go, you aren't a bad guy' and adds that Danny never hurt anyone but himself.
Danny responds with guilt about Bobby and his family, indicating the comment had emotional weight.

memories.memory_text: After handing over the envelope, I told him to take the money and later asked how he even got into this mess—though I didn't push when he shut it down.
grounded_facts: Tanner seals cash in an envelope and slides it to Danny, who initially doesn't touch it.
Tanner says 'Now take the money,' which prompts Danny to stuff the envelope into his jacket.
Tanner later asks how Danny got into this position, showing restrained curiosity, but does not press further after Danny begins responding.

memories.memory_text: I heard the voice through the earpiece—weak, filtered—Danny saying he didn't have a choice. Then Bubba's threat, cold and sharp. Garcetti looked at me like we'd both just stepped on something solid. I told him yeah, this could be the one.
grounded_facts: Tanner is positioned in an alley with Garcetti across the street from a burned-out building.
Tanner wears an earpiece connected to a parabolic microphone used to capture distant audio.
Tanner hears Danny and Bubba's conversation as filtered, broken audio through the earpiece.
Garcetti removes his earpiece and asks Tanner if he's thinking the same thing.
Tanner responds that this could be the suspect they are looking for.

memories.memory_text: I called Danny a liar, straight out — no warning. He tried to play it off, but I could feel the shift, like the air in the car got heavier. That’s when I knew I was the one in control now.
grounded_facts: Tanner says 'Bullshit. He's a liar.' in response to Danny's behavior.
The scene notes state that 'The dynamic has suddenly changed in the trio's relationship. Tanner is now the bad cop.'
Tanner follows up with 'You think you're so god damned smart, don't you?' and 'Well, you played the wrong mark this time, asshole.'

memories.memory_text: I told Danny Pooh-Bear doesn't run a lab — he hits other dealers. That’s when the kid realized he wasn’t setting up a buy, he was walking into a robbery.
grounded_facts: Tanner states Pooh-Bear does not have a drug lab
Tanner explains Pooh-Bear robs other drug dealers
Danny expresses concern about being robbed

memories.memory_text: I told him the red car was registered to a teacher — Mrs. Nancy Plummer. The look on his face... like he’d seen a ghost. Not the monster he expected.
grounded_facts: Tanner confirms the red car is registered to Mrs. Nancy Plummer
Mrs. Plummer is identified as a 57-year-old third-grade teacher
Danny reacts strongly to the name

memories.memory_text: I held up the test kit—blue. We were in business. No doubt about it.
grounded_facts: Tanner conducted a chemical test on the meth using a field kit.
The test result turned blue, indicating a positive result.
Tanner stated, 'We're in business,' confirming the meth was confirmed.

memories.memory_text: I pulled up Bubba's file—fifty-seven bullets into an informant. Said it straight. No flinching.
grounded_facts: Tanner accessed an FBI criminal database and retrieved Bubba's mug shot and record.
Tanner revealed Bubba pumped fifty-seven bullets into a police informant.
Tanner delivered the information directly and without emotional reaction.

memories.memory_text: I remember the yearbook photo—balding, red hair with a white streak, criminology listed under my name. Someone was looking, digging.
grounded_facts: Tanner's photo appears in the El Camino College yearbook for the class of '84.
The photo shows him as balding with red hair and a streak of white.
His major, criminology, is listed beneath the photo.

memories.memory_text: I saw my name on that alumni list—Tanner, Gus, Class of '84. Detective, Gardena Police Department. Didn't expect to see it pop up like that.
grounded_facts: The character's full name is Tanner, Gus.
He graduated in 1984 from El Camino College.
He is a detective with the Gardena Police Department.
His name appears in an alumni listing viewed on a library computer.

memories.memory_text: I felt the jolt from behind—barely a bump, but wrong. Too sudden, too clean. I turned and saw the beer cans on his front seat, Danny just sitting there like it was routine.
grounded_facts: Tanner was in a car that was rear-ended at low speed.
Danny was driving the car that hit Tanner's vehicle.
Empty beer cans were visible on the front seat of Danny's car.
The collision occurred suddenly and appeared suspicious to Tanner.

memories.memory_text: We cuffed the dealer and hauled him out clean—no resistance, no mistakes. Another textbook arrest, nothing more.
grounded_facts: Tanner participated in arresting a dope dealer alongside Garcetti.
The arrest was conducted according to procedure, as noted by Danny's observation that 'they did everything by the book.'
The event took place at night outside an apartment building.

memories.memory_text: I stood on the rooftop, watching Battle walk into the building—this call might actually be the break we’ve been waiting for.
grounded_facts: Tanner received an anonymous tip via a confidential informant, relayed through TRAHN.
Tanner was on a rooftop at night with Garcetti, observing Battle enter a building.
Tanner said, 'This could be the one we're looking for,' indicating cautious hope.

memories.memory_text: I was sifting through the trunk and saw the sawed-off shotgun, two semi-automatic handguns, knives, gloves, and ski-masks stuffed inside.
grounded_facts: Tanner sifting through the trunk
Trunk contains a sawed-off shotgun, two semi-automatic handguns, two knives, gloves, and ski-masks

memories.memory_text: Garcetti was putting on a phony license plate while I stood by the car, the kind we’ve never used before.
grounded_facts: Garcetti putting a phony license plate
Car is unfamiliar to Tanner
Tanner present at scene during plate installation

memories.memory_text: I saw the headlights first through the night vision—steady, slow. Then the rental car came into view, Danny behind the wheel. He’s here.
grounded_facts: Tanner is using night vision glasses to observe.
Danny arrives at the location in a rental car.
The observation occurs at night from a bluff overlooking Pooh-Bear's compound.
Tanner verbally confirms Danny's arrival with the phrase 'Here he comes.'

memories.memory_text: I was shot in the shoulder by Danny. I fell to the floor and looked up at him, realizing it was him.
grounded_facts: Tanner is shot in the shoulder by Danny
Tanner collapses to the floor after being shot
Tanner sees Danny standing over him with a gun

memories.memory_text: I was on the floor after being shot. Danny stood over me. Then the syringe went into my neck.
grounded_facts: Tanner is on the floor after being shot in the shoulder
Danny stands over Tanner after shooting him
A syringe is inserted into Tanner's neck

memories.memory_text: I said my name was Tom Van Allen, or maybe Danny Flynne—I don’t know, the names blurred together, and I could feel the fire eating through me, through the money, through the photograph of her smile.
grounded_facts: The character speaks his name as Tom Van Allen or Danny Flynne.
He expresses uncertainty about his identity with 'I don't know anymore.'
The room is on fire, including a duffel bag of money and a photograph inside a trumpet case.
He addresses an unseen observer, asking them to decide who he was.

memories.memory_text: I told whoever was listening to decide what I was—trumpet player, speed freak, avenging angel, none of it mattered now, the card with 'CONGRATULATIONS!' was burning on the floor and I was just trying to make sense of it before the end.
grounded_facts: The character lists multiple possible identities: avenging angel, Judas, husband, prodigal son, prince of Denmark, trumpet player, speed freak.
He repeatedly says 'You decide, friend' to an unseen observer.
A greeting card with 'CONGRATULATIONS!' and a blood stain inside is shown on the floor, blown open by fire's wind.
He attempts to narrate his story before dying, starting with 'a little background on the mad world of the tweaker.'

memories.memory_text: I told Tanner to be careful, not because I care about him, but because I was actually worried about that kid back there.
grounded_facts: Tom (as Danny) says 'Hey Tanner ... you be careful, okay?'
Tom (as Danny) follows up with 'Don't be. I'm worried about the kid.'

memories.memory_text: I played 'Saeta' on my trumpet as the sun set, and when Liz said it hurt her heart, I joked about being a virtuoso—then she teased me, saying Miles had the hot ass, not me.
grounded_facts: Tom played 'Saeta' on the trumpet at Salton Sea during sunset.
Liz commented that the music was melancholy and emotionally affecting.
Tom asked for feedback on his performance and responded playfully to Liz's teasing.
Liz made a joke about Miles Davis having a hot ass, not Tom.

memories.memory_text: I tackled her into the sand and kept kissing her, but she laughed and pushed me off, yelling 'Tom! Gross!'—then I spooned her and told her the song's sadness comes from imagining life without her.
grounded_facts: Tom playfully tackled Liz to the ground after her joke.
Tom kissed Liz repeatedly while she laughed and protested.
Liz pushed Tom off and said 'Tom! Gross!' in response to his affection.
Tom later spooned Liz in the sand and shared that he thinks of life without her when playing the song sadly.

memories.memory_text: I remember saying 'Come on, Liz. We'll get eaten alive,' but my voice didn't feel like mine—it was like I was fading, and no one could really hear me anymore.
grounded_facts: Tom's voice is heard in the projected image on the wall, delivering the line 'Come on, Liz. We'll get eaten alive.'
The scene shows Tom's presence as part of a fading memory projection, not a physical or active participant.
Tom's dialogue occurs within a remembered moment on the beach, now being recalled by Danny.

memories.memory_text: I remember leaning forward, squinting through the windshield at that lone house glowing up the hill, and saying I’d go ask for directions—after Liz told me to stop fucking around.
grounded_facts: The character (Danny impersonating Tom) decides to approach a house to ask for directions after being criticized by Liz.
The decision follows Liz's frustration and the character's earlier joke about being eaten.
The house is visible up ahead through the windshield in a remote area near the Salton Sea at night.

memories.memory_text: I snapped at Liz, telling her how tired I was of being reminded what a loser I am every time I messed up, and then threw in that bit about her having the steady job while I'm just an unemployed musician—like I had to hand her the knife to twist it deeper.
grounded_facts: Tom says, 'No. You know what gets old? Being reminded what a loser you are every time you screw up.'
Tom says, 'But that's okay, Liz, you're the one with the steady job, you pay all the bills. I'm just an unemployed musician.'

memories.memory_text: After she said not to take my low self-esteem out on her, I didn't respond—just kept driving, looking ahead, and stayed silent when she called my name.
grounded_facts: Liz says, 'So don't take your low self-esteem out on me.'
Tom (as Danny) does not respond after this line.
Liz says, '...Tom... screw it. Tell me when you're ready to apologize.'
Tom remains silent and stares ahead while driving.

memories.memory_text: I felt the cold air hit my skin when Little Bill yanked my pants down, and then I saw it—the weasel thrashing in the cage, eyes wild, and I knew I was going to have to put myself inside that thing or get shot.
grounded_facts: Little Bill dropped Danny's (Tom's) trousers.
The cage contained a gigantic crazed weasel.
Tom was forced to lower his pants under threat of death.
Big Bill pointed a flintlock pistol at Tom's head.

memories.memory_text: While they were shoving me toward the cage, I pressed something into the lining of Little Bill's jacket—don't even remember what it was now, just that I did it fast, like a reflex, hoping they wouldn't notice.
grounded_facts: Tom was escorted by the Bills toward the cage.
Tom secretly placed an object on Little Bill during the confrontation.

memories.memory_text: I remember standing there, hands shaking, when Verne slammed the table and said I was spitting on Liz’s grave with my drug use—like I hadn’t thought about it a thousand times already.
grounded_facts: Tom (referred to as Danny in the scene) is confronted about his drug use by Verne and Nancy.
Verne accuses Tom of disgracing Liz's memory through his drug use and implies he might be buying from her killers.
Tom does not deny the accusation but says it's complicated and that they don’t understand him.

memories.memory_text: I called Liz my wife in front of that creepy guy, and she didn’t say anything—just gave me this tight look before telling me to make it fast.
grounded_facts: Tom (Danny) refers to Liz as 'my wife' during a conversation with Bo.
Liz responds with discomfort, saying 'Try to make it fast' after the exchange.
The moment occurs while they are at Bo's house, preparing to leave.

memories.memory_text: I sat there with all those agents staring at me, feeling exposed when Trahn said my real name and that my wife had been murdered—like I was no longer just Danny Flynne, but someone they were supposed to trust with a badge.
grounded_facts: Tom Van Allen is revealed to be the same person as Danny Flynne.
Tom's wife was murdered at a meth lab near the Salton Sea about a year and a half ago.
The FBI agents are in a conference room being briefed by Special Agent Tex Trahn.
Tom feels self-conscious during the revelation.
An agent questions how a civilian could pull off such an operation.
Tom responds by saying 'I played the long shot. Just got lucky.'

memories.memory_text: I told him my name—Tom Van Allen—standing there with the gun, watching Tanner frown like he wasn’t sure if I was real or just some ghost from the past.
grounded_facts: Tom reveals his real name, Tom Van Allen, to Tanner.
Tanner frowns in response to the revelation.

memories.memory_text: I shot him in the shoulder and made him lie there, asking over and over what he would do—die a hero or save himself—while he just stared, too scared or too broken to answer.
grounded_facts: Tom shoots Tanner in the shoulder.
Tom forces Tanner to imagine a scenario where he must choose between saving his wife or surviving.
Tom repeatedly demands an answer from Tanner, who remains silent.

memories.memory_text: I said my name was Tom Van Allen, or maybe Danny Flynne—I didn’t know anymore, and I could feel the fire closing in, so I just let it go, asked the unseen voice to decide who I was.
grounded_facts: The character speaks in voiceover, uncertain of his identity, offering both 'Tom Van Allen' and 'Danny Flynne' as possible names.
He states 'I don't know anymore' and invites an unseen presence to define him, saying 'Maybe you can help me, friend.'
The room is on fire, the trumpet case photo is burning, and money is alight on the bed, creating a sense of urgency.

memories.memory_text: I picked 'speed freak'—not because it was true, but because it was as good a label as any left, and then I started to explain the mad world of the tweaker before everything faded.
grounded_facts: After listing possible identities, the character settles on 'Speed freak' after a pause, saying 'That's as good a place as any.'
He transitions directly into offering background on 'the mad world of the tweaker,' indicating a narrative shift.
This occurs in voiceover as the scene fades out, surrounded by fire and destruction.

memories.memory_text: I told Tanner to be careful, and when he tried to make a joke of it, I said I was worried about the kid — I meant it, even if I didn’t say it soft.
grounded_facts: Tom (Danny) says 'Hey Tanner ... you be careful, okay?'
When Tanner responds sarcastically, Danny clarifies 'Don't be. I'm worried about the kid.'

memories.memory_text: Garcetti threw me against the car and got nose-to-nose. I told him his teeth were fucking perfect. He let me go.
grounded_facts: Garcetti throws Danny against the car and confronts him aggressively.
Garcetti gets nose-to-nose with Danny during the confrontation.
Danny says 'Garcetti, you're teeth, they're fucking perfect.'
Garcetti releases Danny and walks away after the comment.

memories.memory_text: I tackled Liz into the sand and just started kissing her all over, and she was laughing, pushing me off, saying 'Tom! Gross!'—probably because of my wiener breath from that hot dog.
grounded_facts: Tom tackled Liz playfully on the beach.
Tom kissed Liz repeatedly while she laughed and pushed him away.
Liz complained about Tom's 'wiener breath' from eating a hot dog.

memories.memory_text: After playing 'Saeta' on my trumpet, I told Liz that I make the song melancholy by thinking about what my life would be like without her—and she just smiled and snuggled closer.
grounded_facts: Tom played 'Saeta' on the trumpet at Salton Sea during sunset.
Tom confessed that he channels thoughts of life without Liz to infuse emotion into the music.
Liz responded by smiling and snuggling closer to Tom.

memories.memory_text: I remember squinting through the windshield, seeing that lone house up ahead, and saying I’d go ask for directions—after Liz told me to stop fucking around.
grounded_facts: Tom is driving the car and is referred to as Danny in the script, but the scene note clarifies he is known as Tom.
Liz expresses frustration about not asking for directions earlier.
Tom makes a joke about being eaten before agreeing to stop joking and approach the house.
Tom spots the house while looking through the windshield and announces he will go ask for directions.

memories.memory_text: I admitted I was wrong just to shut her up, but it felt like swallowing glass—she loves hearing it, and I hate that I gave it to her.
grounded_facts: Tom says
Okay, Liz, I was wrong. There. You happy?" after being pressured to admit fault.", His tone is sarcastic and resentful, indicating reluctance in admitting the mistake.

memories.memory_text: I snapped and threw my unemployment in her face—said I was just a loser musician while she pays the bills, and it hung there, ugly and true.
grounded_facts: Tom says
But that's okay, Liz, you're the one with the steady job, you pay all the bills. I'm just an unemployed musician. You have every right."
This statement expresses resentment about his financial role and self-worth in the relationship.

memories.memory_text: I remember standing there frozen when Pooh-Bear told me to pull my pants down, and then Big Bill shoved that flintlock in my face like it was some fucked-up joke.
grounded_facts: Pooh-Bear commanded Danny to pull his pants down
Big Bill produced a flintlock pistol and pointed it at Danny's head
Danny reacted with nervous laughter and fear

memories.memory_text: Little Bill dropped my pants. Then I saw the hole where Pooh-Bear’s nose should have been.
grounded_facts: Little Bill dropped Danny's pants
Danny saw the gaping hole where Pooh-Bear's nose should have been

memories.memory_text: I remember sitting at their dining table, Nancy grabbing my wrist when I tried to leave, her saying they just wanted to help me, like I was some broken thing they could fix.
grounded_facts: Danny (Tom) is at the Palos Verdes house with Liz's parents, Nancy and Verne.
Nancy stops Danny from leaving by grabbing his wrist.
Nancy says, 'No. We want to help you, Tom.'
Danny is hesitant and avoids eye contact, especially with Verne.

memories.memory_text: I asked if the bathroom was clean, and he said it was 'kinda filthy,' then snapped at me when I tried to talk to Liz—like I was stepping out of line just checking in with my own wife.
grounded_facts: Tom (Danny in disguise) asked about the bathroom and was told it was 'kinda' filthy.
Tom said 'I was talking to my wife' after Bo reacted negatively to him speaking to Liz.
Bo's tone shifted abruptly when Tom tried to engage Liz, creating tension.

memories.memory_text: I remember standing there while Trahn told the room who I really was, my real name spilling out like it was evidence in a case file, and someone asking how a civilian like me could pull it off—like I wasn’t supposed to be there at all.
grounded_facts: Tom Van Allen's alias 'Danny Flynne' is revealed in front of FBI agents.
Trahn discloses that Tom's wife was murdered at a meth lab near the Salton Sea.
An agent expresses surprise that Tom is a civilian.
Tom responds to the agent's question by saying he 'played the long shot' and 'got lucky.'

memories.memory_text: I said my name was Tom, Tom Van Allen, and then I raised my gun and shot Tanner in the shoulder.
grounded_facts: Tom says 'My name is Tom' and 'Tom Van Allen' to Tanner.
Danny raises his gun and shoots Tanner in the shoulder.
Tanner collapses to the floor after being shot.

memories.memory_text: I shot him in the shoulder and stood over him, asking what he would do—die a hero or save himself—repeating the question until he didn't answer.
grounded_facts: Tom shot Tanner in the shoulder and stood over him.
Tom asked Tanner what he would do in a hypothetical situation where he could die trying to save his wife or survive by staying put.
Tom repeated the question 'What do you do?' multiple times while Tanner remained silent.

memories.memory_text: I was looking in the mirror, feeling like Tom for the first time—smoking, standing tall—until that voice said my real name, and then the shot hit my gut.
grounded_facts: Danny (in Tom's clothes) was looking at his reflection while smoking a cigarette, identifying himself as Tom Van Allen.
Quincey revealed Danny's true identity and shot him in the stomach.
The cigarette fell from Danny's mouth and rolled under the bed after being shot.

memories.memory_text: I got my revenge, and then Danny said I was dead—like I never mattered anymore.
grounded_facts: Tom Van Allen's story concludes with Danny declaring that Tom got his revenge.
Danny states that both Tom Van Allen and Danny Flynne are dead, effectively ending Tom's psychological presence.
